changes.detail: All five required fields present and correct. work_id: CONTEXT-MOD-1_questionnaire_and_medication_modifier_governance. branch: work/CONTEXT-MOD-1-questionnaire-and-medication-modifier-governance. risk_level: HIGH. execution_model: TWO_PHASE_START_FINISH. change_type: CONTENT. CONFIRMED: automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md lines 2–6.

HIGH is a conservative over-classification relative to formal SOP v1.3.1 triggers. No Intelligence Core modification occurs; no listed HIGH-risk files (backend/ssot/, backend/core/pipeline/, backend/core/analytics/, control-plane scripts) are modified. HIGH is acceptable and safe — it imposes stricter review requirements without creating any restriction risk. No action required. CONFIRMED: automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md lines 78–85 (governance rationale stated).

CONTENT is correct. All sprint outputs are non-runtime: docs/architecture/ paper, knowledge_bus/governance/ YAML catalogue (runtime_consumed: false), knowledge_bus/schema/ YAML schema, backend/scripts/ standalone validator (not a runtime dependency), backend/tests/regression/ tests. No Intelligence Core files modified. No runtime loaders, evaluators, or pipeline logic touched. Docs-Only Bypass (SOP §11) does NOT apply: artefacts include knowledge_bus/governance/, knowledge_bus/schema/, backend/scripts/, backend/tests/ — all outside /docs/. Bus required. CONFIRMED: automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md lines 332–342 (required artefacts).
TWO_PHASE_START_FINISH correct. Multiple output artefact directories outside /docs/ require bus lifecycle. CONFIRMED: automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md lines 76–77.

All 7 required source paths verified present in repo by glob and content read. (1) docs/architecture/MED-FRAME-1_signal_family_contextual_frame_architecture.md — CONFIRMED EXISTS, content read lines 1–80: non-runtime architecture sprint, defines signal families, frames, activation_key format, 5 illustrative creatinine frames, MED-FRAME-1 line 40-44. (2) docs/audit-papers/MED-FRAME-2_medical_frame_identity_index_report.md — CONFIRMED EXISTS. (3) knowledge_bus/governance/medical_frame_identity_index_v1.yaml — CONFIRMED EXISTS, content read lines 1–115: schema_version 1.0.0, index_id medical_frame_identity_index_v1, 4 creatinine frames present (line 16: frame_creatinine_reduced_glomerular_filtration; line 41: frame_creatinine_legacy_s24_egfr_escalation; line 66: frame_creatinine_legacy_s24_potassium_escalation; line 92: frame_creatinine_pass3_promoted_candidate). (4) knowledge_bus/schema/medical_frame_identity_index_schema_v1.yaml — CONFIRMED EXISTS, content read lines 1–60: LOCKED schema, frame_required_fields confirmed. (5) docs/governance/KNOWLEDGE_BUS_SOP_v1.3.1.md — CONFIRMED EXISTS. (6) docs/governance/KNOWLEDGE_BUS_PASS3_PROMOTION_PROTOCOL_v1.1.md — CONFIRMED EXISTS. (7) docs/sprints/launch_core_carry_forward_register.md — CONFIRMED EXISTS, content read: CF-MEDFRAME1-002 status Open (line 53), notes 'Questionnaire SSOT + intervention_effects_registry exist; frame binding not implemented' — exact motivation for this sprint.

All secondary inspection targets locatable. (1) backend/ssot/questionnaire.json — CONFIRMED EXISTS. (2) backend/ssot/lifestyle_registry.yaml — CONFIRMED EXISTS (established in prior sprints). (3) knowledge_bus/interventions/intervention_effects_registry_v1.yaml — CONFIRMED EXISTS. PATH NOTE: artefact is under knowledge_bus/interventions/ not knowledge_bus/governance/ — Cursor must locate by directory inspection, not assumption. (4) knowledge_bus/schema/intervention_effects_registry_schema_v1.yaml — CONFIRMED EXISTS. (5) backend/artifacts/intervention_effects_registry_audit.md — CONFIRMED EXISTS. (6) backend/core/contracts/intervention_annotation_v1.py — CONFIRMED EXISTS (Intelligence Core — INSPECT ONLY, must not be modified). (7) backend/core/analytics/intervention_annotation_compiler_v1.py — CONFIRMED EXISTS (Intelligence Core — INSPECT ONLY). (8) backend/core/analytics/medication_caveat_assembler_v1.py — CONFIRMED EXISTS (Intelligence Core — INSPECT ONLY). All paths confirmed by glob.

All sprint STOP pre-conditions reviewed against actual repo state. MED-FRAME-1 merged — CONFIRMED: docs/architecture/MED-FRAME-1_signal_family_contextual_frame_architecture.md exists. MED-FRAME-2 merged — CONFIRMED: knowledge_bus/governance/medical_frame_identity_index_v1.yaml (governed, not draft) exists at line 1 schema_version 1.0.0. medical_frame_identity_index_v1.yaml present — CONFIRMED. carry-forward register present — CONFIRMED. CF-MEDFRAME1-002 status Open — CONFIRMED: launch_core_carry_forward_register.md line 53. Existing questionnaire artefacts locatable — CONFIRMED: backend/ssot/questionnaire.json. Existing medication/drug-category artefacts locatable — CONFIRMED: knowledge_bus/interventions/intervention_effects_registry_v1.yaml, backend/core/analytics/medication_caveat_assembler_v1.py. No STOP conditions triggered pre-execution.

The prompt's worked example (lines 283–316) references 4 conceptual creatinine frames. Only 1 has a confirmed governed medical_frame_id in the identity index. (1) 'reduced glomerular filtration' → frame_creatinine_reduced_glomerular_filtration — CONFIRMED EXISTS: medical_frame_identity_index_v1.yaml line 16. (2) 'albuminuric kidney damage' → NO governing medical_frame_id in index. This is a future frame described in MED-FRAME-1 architecture (line 41) but not yet indexed. (3) 'acute electrolyte risk' → closest existing frame is frame_creatinine_legacy_s24_potassium_escalation (medical_frame_identity_index_v1.yaml line 66), but runtime_authority_status: inactive (line 87) and collision_status: requires_adjudication (line 88) — labels diverge from the prompt's 'acute electrolyte risk' framing. (4) 'creatinine distortion / muscle-supplement context' → NO governing medical_frame_id in index. IMPLICATION FOR VALIDATOR: Rule #9 states 'Confirm referenced medical_frame_ids exist in medical_frame_identity_index_v1.yaml where specified.' Cursor MUST implement this as a conditional check — validate only when medical_frame_ids field is non-empty/non-null. Catalogue entries for non-indexed frames must use null or empty medical_frame_ids with explanatory notes. If Cursor populates medical_frame_ids with conceptual labels that do not match the index, the validator will reject the catalogue against its own rule #9. REQUIRED ACTION: Cursor must design the catalogue and validator consistently so that: (a) modifier entries for the one confirmed frame reference frame_creatinine_reduced_glomerular_filtration explicitly; (b) modifier entries for non-indexed frames use null/empty medical_frame_ids; (c) validator rule #9 implements the 'where specified' condition strictly as a null-guard. This is not a STOP condition if handled correctly.

Three existing artefacts are Intelligence Core components under backend/core/: backend/core/contracts/intervention_annotation_v1.py, backend/core/analytics/intervention_annotation_compiler_v1.py, backend/core/analytics/medication_caveat_assembler_v1.py. The sprint must INSPECT ONLY — not modify. The runtime boundary section (prompt lines 390–406) states 'Do not modify: SignalEvaluator, SignalRegistry, runtime loaders, domain_score_assembler, report_compiler, frontend, SSOT...' and 'Do not change runtime analysis.' These files are covered by 'runtime loaders' and 'Do not change runtime analysis.' Cursor must not modify any of these files. If any runtime change appears necessary, STOP condition #7 applies. CONFIRMED: backend/core/analytics/medication_caveat_assembler_v1.py and backend/core/contracts/intervention_annotation_v1.py confirmed by glob.

All 4 creatinine frames in medical_frame_identity_index_v1.yaml have context_inputs_supported.questionnaire_modifiers: false and context_inputs_supported.medication_modifiers: false (lines 27-30, 53-56, 79-82, 105-108). This is correct for current sprint — the index must NOT be modified. The modifier catalogue being created in this sprint is non-runtime and does not activate these fields. Future CONTEXT-MOD-2 will need to update the index when modifiers are bound into Layer B frame assembly. The audit report must note this alignment dependency for the next sprint. No action required in CONTEXT-MOD-1.

The new validate_context_modifier_catalogue.py must NOT import from any runtime pipeline module: no SignalEvaluator, no SignalRegistry, no backend/core/analytics imports, no runtime loaders. It must be a standalone YAML loader and schema validation script, consistent with the pattern established by validate_medical_frame_identity_index.py. The validator's reference to medical_frame_identity_index_v1.yaml must be via direct YAML file load — not via any runtime loader. STOP condition #4: 'validator would require runtime imports.' Cursor must confirm runtime independence at Stage 4A closure. CONFIRMED requirement: automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md lines 358–370.

8 regression test targets fully specified in prompt (lines 374–382): (1) valid catalogue passes; (2) duplicate modifier_id fails; (3) unknown modifier_type fails; (4) unknown modifier_effect fails; (5) unknown allowed_layer fails; (6) runtime_consumed true fails; (7) referenced frame IDs must exist where specified; (8) modifier catalogue is non-runtime. All 8 are testable against static YAML fixtures without runtime state. Test design for #7 must be consistent with FINDING in check 2b_worked_example_frame_id_gap — the fixture should exercise the 'where specified' null-guard.

Out-of-scope list is comprehensive (automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md lines 474–486): no modifier evaluation implemented, no questionnaire schema altered, no medication schema altered, no Layer B frame assembly altered, no runtime analysis changed, no frontend changed, no SSOT changed, no direct clinical rules to SignalEvaluator, no creatinine authority adjudicated, no bulk frame indexing. STOP condition #7 protects against inadvertent runtime changes. Scope creep risk is LOW.

CF-MEDFRAME1-002 must be resolved only when all 10 success criteria are met (prompt lines 549–560). CF-MEDFRAME1-003 (creatinine adjudication) remains Open — not affected by this sprint. New carry-forward CF-CONTEXT-MOD-2 must be added for binding governed context modifiers into Layer B frame assembly (prompt lines 438–440). No other carry-forwards are affected. CONFIRMED: launch_core_carry_forward_register.md line 53 (CF-MEDFRAME1-002 Open) and line 54 (CF-MEDFRAME1-003 Open).

advisories.title: Validator rule #9 must implement 'where specified' as a strict null-guard
advisories.detail: The worked example references 4 conceptual creatinine frames, but only frame_creatinine_reduced_glomerular_filtration has a governed medical_frame_id in the identity index. The other 3 conceptual frames have no matching frame_id. Cursor must author both the catalogue and validator so that: (a) medical_frame_ids with non-null values are validated against the index; (b) null/empty medical_frame_ids are accepted without validation. If this guard is absent, every catalogue entry for a non-indexed frame will fail rule #9 and cause STOP condition #3. This is a design consistency requirement, not a flaw in the prompt.

advisories.title: intervention_effects_registry_v1.yaml path is knowledge_bus/interventions/ not knowledge_bus/governance/
advisories.detail: The actual path is knowledge_bus/interventions/intervention_effects_registry_v1.yaml. Prompt does not give a specific path — Cursor may assume governance/ by analogy with the identity index. Must locate by directory inspection. No impact on sprint outcomes, but misreporting the path in the audit report would be incorrect.

advisories.title: Acute electrolyte risk frame: use existing frame_id with label disambiguation note
advisories.detail: frame_creatinine_legacy_s24_potassium_escalation (medical_frame_identity_index_v1.yaml line 66) is the closest existing frame to the prompt's 'acute electrolyte risk' concept. It is runtime_authority_status: inactive and requires_adjudication. If Cursor references this frame in the worked example, it must use the governed medical_frame_id exactly and note in the catalogue that label disambiguation is pending clinical adjudication of the legacy s24 potassium frame.
